Adding Formatted Attributes to Leaderboards

Leaderboards can have zero or more formatted attributes. A formatted attribute is an attribute whose information is calculated dynamically when the player views the leaderboard. When you add a formatted attribute to a leaderboard, you specify the formula used to calculate the attribute's value.

Note    Neither XLAST nor the XTL places a limit on the number of formatted attributes a leaderboard contains. It is left to the developer to decide how many formatted attributes are appropriate for a leaderboard.

Use the following procedure to add a formatted attribute to a leaderboard.

  1. Right-click the leaderboard to which you would like to add a formatted attribute. In the menu that appears, click Add Formatted Attributes.
  2. XLAST displays the Add Formatted Attributes dialog box. Enter the number of attributes to create. The default is 1.
  3. If you are creating just one attribute, enter its ID. If you are creating a group of attributes, enter the ID of the first attribute in the group.
  4. You can optionally use the drop-down list to select an attribute to use as a template. This enables you to create one attribute, configure it, and then use it to create more attributes with the same characteristics.
  5. Click OK when you are ready to create the attribute(s)..
  6. Click the new attribute to select it, and then it again to edit its name. Set the name of the attribute to something meaningful to you. Players do not see this name.
  7. Use the Return Type drop-down list to specify the type of the attribute's data. Formatted attributes can have a return types of Integer, Float, Percent, Gamertag, TimeStamp, Milliseconds, TenMilliseconds, HundredMilliseconds, Seconds, Minutes, Hours, Days, String, or Team.

    Note    The selections TenMilliseconds and HundredMilliseconds mean that the value specified is in tens and hundreds of milliseconds, respectively. So setting the return type to TenMilliseconds and returning a value of 2 indicates a time span of 20 milliseconds.

  8. Use the Locale drop-down list to select a locale. Use the edit box immediately to the right to enter the attribute's localized name. This is the attribute name that players see when they look at the leaderboard. To support additional locales, click the root node of the project tree and click the check boxes in the Supported Locales list for the additional locales you want to support on your leaderboard.

  9. Enter a formula into the formatted attribute's Formula box. The formula can be any C# formula, with certain restrictions. The C# expression can use Attr[i] to refer to the leaderboard's base attributes. It can use Gamertag(PUID) to resolve a Gamertag from a PUID, and any C# expression operators except: new, assignment, increment/decrement (++/--), function invocation, accessing properties on any object other than this, attr[i], or its contents. Formulas can use the Math class. They can also contain the following Stats functions:

    Function Name Function Prototype
    FormatTime protected string FormatTime(object AttributeId, string FormatString);
    AddLong protected long AddLong(object Val1, object Val2);
    SubtractLong protected long SubtractLong(object Val1, object Val2);
    MultiplyLong protected long MultiplyLong(object Val1, object Val2);
    DivideLong protected long DivideLong(object Val1, object Val2);
    AddDouble protected double AddDouble(object Val1, object Val2);
    SubtractDouble protected double SubtractDouble(object Val1, object Val2);
    MultiplyDouble protected double MultiplyDouble(object Val1, object Val2);
    DivideDouble protected double DivideDouble(object Val1, object Val2);
    SubInt protected int SubInt(int AttributeId, int StartBit, int NumOfBytes);
